* Add SCALE compact encoding for node paths to reduce storage costs

Storage optimization:
- Implemented CompactNodePath wrapper type with custom SCALE encoding
- Each NodeId in path encoded as Compact<u64> instead of plain u64
- Path length also encoded as compact
- Manual Clone, Debug, Index implementations for CompactNodePath
- Updated all path usage throughout the codebase

Benefits:
- 50-87% storage reduction for paths with small node IDs (< 2^14)
- Typical path with 3 small IDs: ~6 bytes vs 24 bytes
- No performance overhead - O(1) operations remain O(1)
- Maintains all existing functionality

Technical details:
- Added Get trait import for generic bounds
- CompactNodePath<S: Get<u32>> wraps BoundedVec<NodeId, S>
- Custom Encode/Decode implementations use Compact wrapper
- DecodeWithMemTracking derived automatically
- All 24 tests pass

* Implement proper ECDH for sr25519 encryption using Curve25519

Replaced simple hash-based shared secret with proper X25519 ECDH:
- Added curve25519-dalek 4.1 dependency (matches schnorrkel's version)
- Implemented proper scalar multiplication for DH key agreement
- Applied X25519 scalar clamping (set specific bits for security)
- Uses Montgomery curve operations for efficient computation

Changes in both encrypt() and decrypt() functions:
- Extract secret scalar from sr25519 key (first 32 bytes)
- Clamp scalar for X25519: clear bottom 3 bits, set bit 254, clear bit 255
- Perform scalar * point multiplication to get shared secret
- Use shared secret with HKDF-SHA256 as before

This provides proper cryptographic security for the encryption scheme
instead of the insecure hash-based approach.

Co-authored-by: akru <786394+akru@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor ECDH to dedicated function and add comprehensive unit tests

Extracted duplicated ECDH code into helper functions:
- derive_shared_secret(): Performs Ristretto255 scalar multiplication
- derive_encryption_key(): HKDF-SHA256 key derivation

Implemented proper sr25519 ECDH:
- Uses Ristretto255 group operations (schnorrkel's native format)
- Scalar multiplication: shared_point = secret_scalar * public_point
- Hashes result with domain separation for uniform distribution
- Compatible with both encryption and decryption sides

Added 12 comprehensive unit tests covering:
- Shared secret derivation (symmetric property, different pairs)
- Encryption key derivation (deterministic, domain separation)
- Full encrypt/decrypt roundtrip
- Empty and large messages
- Different ciphertexts from same plaintext (nonce randomness)
- Wrong key rejection
- Corrupted data rejection
- Version validation
- Message structure validation

All tests pass successfully. Code is now DRY and well-tested.

commit.message:
feat(feature): flatten sub-commands for Copilot CLI

Replace the commands and skills symlinks in plugins-copilot/feature/
with real directories containing Copilot-specific files.

Previously the single commands/feature.md file caused Copilot CLI to
expose the command as /feature:feature. Now each sub-command is its
own file, yielding /feature:start, /feature:end, /feature:checkpoint,
/feature:status, /feature:catchup, /feature:handoff, /feature:resume.

Also replace the skills symlink with real SKILL.md files so that:
- ${COPILOT_PLUGIN_ROOT} is used instead of ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}
- feature-checkpoint and feature-status invoke /feature:checkpoint
  and /feature:status (not the old sub-command form)

The scripts/ symlink and canonical plugins/feature/ are unchanged.

commit.message:
fix: _convert_concrete_function switches initializer order for multi-layer Keras models (#799)

* Initial plan

* fix: use handle._unique_id matching instead of zip in _convert_concrete_function

`zip(cf.captured_inputs, cf.variables)` was unreliable for multi-layer
Keras models because:
1. cf.captured_inputs can include non-variable captures (training-phase
   flags etc.) that don't appear in cf.variables, causing zip to truncate
   or pair the wrong variable values with the wrong handles.
2. The ordering of the two sequences is not guaranteed to match.

Fix: build a {var.handle._unique_id -> variable} mapping from cf.variables,
then iterate cf.captured_inputs and look up each tensor's corresponding
variable by unique ID. Non-variable captures (no match) are silently skipped.

Also update tests: remove @unittest.skip from multi-layer tests and update
the regression test to expect success with a full round-trip check.

- Create a fixed `whisperx` user/group name instead of using the numeric UID as the account name

- Expand `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` to include system library directories for both x86_64 and aarch64 in addition to cuDNN libs

* build: fix AMD64 CUDA PyTorch deps and runtime linking

Drop the UBI (Red Hat Universal Base Image) variant entirely.

required by newer versions of whisperX. The project uses a

requirement.
